utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so hi zachary good to see you today so i'm looking at my notes i see you coming in with some right elbow pain and you also have a past medical history of hypertension diabetes we're gon na check on those so can you tell me what happened to your elbow

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i just woke up one day it was hurting

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it was hurting okay how long ago would you say that was

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: since

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: since yesterday alright

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's actually my it's actually my left elbow my left arm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay they must have written that down wrong so it is your your left arm are you right handed or left handed

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: right handed

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so do you think you might have slept on it wrong or anything like that or what

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so what part of your elbow would you say hurts

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: from my elbow all the way up to the to the neck

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: elbow up to the neck alright is it your entire elbow or or would you say a a individual part of it is painful

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: just from the elbow up to the neck all the way up the arm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all the way up the arm alright

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: or all the way down the arm to the elbow

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and what would you describe the pain as is it like a stabbing pain is it numb what what's going on

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: kind of a achiness achiness heaviness

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: achiness okay did you do anything yesterday that you think might have caused the pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: just a just a usual day of working around the house

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay do you remember i know sometimes do chores you end up lifting heavy things do you remember doing that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no no no nothing nothing really unusual you know mowed grass and that's about it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright have you taken anything for the pain yet

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: some motrin

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and would you say that worked for you

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not really still kinda comes and goes and aches

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright so we'll we'll take a look at your your arm there because it's like your entire left arm up from your shoulder to your elbow does hurt in a second we'll do an exam of that so i see you're also coming in we're gon na check on your hypertension and you came in today your blood pressure is a little bit high it was one fifty over ninety i do n't think we talked last time about us getting you a blood pressure cuff so you could take those and keep track and and and and let us know so what have your blood pressures been running lately

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: one twenty five over seventy two

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay that's not

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: average

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that's not too bad you might have a little white coat syndrome you seeing me today because it's a little bit high today so you're on that two . five of norvasc have you been taking that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay that's good how about your diet how is that going i know you were having a little trouble before your sot intake was a little bit high

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i think i'm doing okay so

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright so as far as your diabetes your last time you came in let's say look at your a1c result it was a seven . two you were on the five hundred of metformin so what have your blood sugars been running

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do n't really check them

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you do n't check them okay so yeah we can get a a glucose test today and and and just check those because that's something we want to do everyday you know just to make sure you're eating the correct amount and that your sugars are are in range to make sure that and also to make sure that our medication is is the right amount as well so we'll do that that glucose test at the end of your visit and we could see if we can adjust your medication or not so let's go ahead and do a physical exam on you so i just want to double check make sure are you having any chest pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah yeah yeah on and off so it kinda comes with the elbow pain arm pain

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay comes with elbow pain arm pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: chest pain

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: chest pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it kinda goes up my neck sometimes too

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: up your neck alright

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah it's that whole side it just aches comes and goes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: comes and goes alright how often is that chest pain happening to you you said it's not constant

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it just started yesterday

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so every hour every fifteen minutes would you say

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do n't know mostly i had to stop mowing grass the other day it bothered me a little bit but but other than that it just kinda comes and goes i guess a few times a day

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright have you had any shortness of breath

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no okay just the chest pain alright

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so i'm gon na listen to your heart here and it it does seem like i do hear a slight murmur with your heart here and i i do n't think we've we've heard that before so i think i'm gon na get an ekg as well because i'm concerned you might be having like a mild heart attack so because you have the pain all the way up your elbow that go into your neck and your your chest pain as well i do n't think it's it's it's musculoskeletal pain because you said you had n't done anything with it so far so what i wan na do is and let's talk a little bit about my assessment and plan here so i i do think that you or you do have that chest pain i'm concerned that you know you might be having a mild heart attack i'm gon na get ekg for that we're also gon na get you some aspirin is a nitroglycerin just to see if that helps with that pain as far as your for your diabetes i want you we're gon na get that p oct glucose and we'll keep you on be five hundred of metformin for now and then for your hypertension we'll keep you on that two . five of of norvasc but i think our most immediate issue is the is the chest pain and so we'll do the ekg and depending upon those results we'll we'll call nine one one because we really need to get you to the hospital if that is the case so how does that sound

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do n't know i was talking to my brother and he's a he is a heart doctor and he kinda thought maybe it was n't the heart but

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: he he thought maybe i had he thought maybe i had something jobie had you know they took jobie to the hospital

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so you heard you heard that did you

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i did n't hear about that no

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah he he he yeah he could n't stop the

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay could stop doing what

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: he could n't stop pooping

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no well yeah i did n't hear about joe biting and and his his not ability his ability did not stop pooping probably had a diarrhea i'm not sure i did n't hear about that but most of the time

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no it could it could stop pooping rushing

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: could not poop and you said pooping

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i know

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it could n't stop pooping yeah i mean that that might cause like anxiety or something like that you know we ca n't stop pooten he's he's he's in charge of my united states but right now i'm i'm really you know me i'm concerned about your heart so i do wan na get the ekg just to make sure you know it'll be would n't be good if we did n't get that you know in the case that you you leave here something terrible happened and and i would n't want that to happen on my watch so

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: right

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: we'll definitely get those tests

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: appreciate it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah no problem we'll get those tests and my nurse will come in and we'll we'll we'll take it from there does that sound good

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay alright thank you so much

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright thanks

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 45-year-old male with a history of hypertension and diabetes.
Left arm pain
The doctor asks the patient to describe the onset of his arm pain.
The pain began when the patient woke up yesterday.
The patient clarifies the pain is in his left arm.
The doctor asks if the patient is right-handed or left-handed.
The patient is right-handed.
The patient reports pain from his left elbow up to his neck.
The patient describes the pain as an achiness and heaviness.
The patient denies any specific injury and reports mowing the grass.
The patient has been taking Motrin for the pain.
The patient reports that Motrin does not provide relief.
Hypertension
The patient's blood pressure is 150/90 today.
The doctor asks what the patient's blood pressure has been running lately.
The patient reports his blood pressure has been around 125/72.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking 2.5 mg of Norvasc.
The patient confirms he is taking 2.5 mg of Norvasc.
Diabetes
The patient's last A1c was 7.2 and he takes 500 mg of metformin.
The doctor asks what the patient's blood sugars have been running.
The patient does not check his blood glucose levels.
The doctor orders a glucose test.
The doctor asks if the patient is having any chest pain.
The patient reports intermittent chest pain that radiates to his neck.
The patient reports the chest pain started yesterday and occurred while mowing the grass.
The doctor asks if the patient has any shortness of breath.
The patient denies shortness of breath.
The doctor detects a slight heart murmur.
The doctor orders an EKG, aspirin, and nitroglycerin.
The doctor assesses the patient for a potential mild heart attack.
